_meta.description: Editable content for FBX deck. Edit via /admin/ or directly. Slide HTML reads keys via data-content attributes; localStorage overrides this file at runtime.

01-cover.lede: A franchise-powered, kit-of-parts operating system for factory-built housing — engineered to scale linearly in cost and exponentially in output.

02-thesis.p1.label: First Mile
02-thesis.p1.title: Get the contract.
02-thesis.p1.body: Marketing, design, sales, permits — vertically integrated. Think Tesla, Carmax, Carvana, but for a delivered home.
02-thesis.p2.label: Warehouse · Distribution Hub
02-thesis.p2.title: Aggregate the kit.
02-thesis.p2.body: Regulatory approvals, structural panels, bath pods, SIPs — sourced, warehoused, and shipped to the Last Mile at scale.
02-thesis.p3.label: Last Mile Network
02-thesis.p3.title: Deliver the home.
02-thesis.p3.body: A trained, equipped franchise network of regional assemblers and installers — the layer no one else has built.

03-trends.trend.1: Sprawl is expensive — capital is shifting to PHX, Utah, Texas.
03-trends.trend.2: Urban infill is hot — ADUs and density development.
03-trends.trend.3: Starter homes in high demand — sub-$300K per door.
03-trends.trend.4: Factory demand from large national builders.
03-trends.trend.5: National & California laws now permit factory-built homes.
03-trends.block1.title: Modular is not new.
03-trends.block1.body: And modular's track record is the definition of insanity.
03-trends.block2.title: Factory production is not new.
03-trends.block2.body: Cars, phones, food — everything is built in a factory. If the trend is smaller, faster, more efficient, the answer is a factory.
03-trends.principle.label: Operating Principle
03-trends.principle.text: R&D and infrastructure spend grows linearly. Output grows exponentially.

04-opportunity.lede: Spool up, empower, and equip small capable craftsmen within 150 miles of every delivery market in America.
04-opportunity.quote.text:
"HomeFab is the layer
the rest of the industry
forgot to build."
04-opportunity.card1.title: Franchisee Model
04-opportunity.card1.body: Capital, systems, orders, and support — delivered to small builders who want to scale within their region.
04-opportunity.card2.title: Approved Installers
04-opportunity.card2.body: A trained, vetted sub-contractor network — empowered to install product and care for the customer.
04-opportunity.card3.title: Logistics
04-opportunity.card3.body: Equipment and movers trained to place panels and pods exactly where installers need them, when they need them.
04-opportunity.card4.title: On-Site Assembly Unlock
04-opportunity.card4.body: Systems that allow 100% of land — including infill and difficult sites — to be served by prefabricated product.

05-why.headline: Why?
05-why.argument: Because we already have a retail market and a distribution hub. The only thing missing from factory-built construction is a robust, well-trained, empowered network of installers for delivery.
05-why.card1.title: Retail Market
05-why.card1.body: Drive demand up for the prefabricated factory housing industry.
05-why.card2.title: Warehouse / Distribution Hub
05-why.card2.body: Off-site prefab of panels and pods enables faster assembly and reduces on-site resource consumption.
05-why.card3.title: Delivery & Installation Network
05-why.card3.body: Leverages local craftsman talent that wants to scale — by giving them capital, systems, orders, and support.

06-momentum.lede: Defensibility doesn't exist on Day 0 — it emerges after a startup has already grown. Move fast, gain users, build leverage. The moat shows up once the conditions for it actually exist.
06-momentum.step1.title: Momentum
06-momentum.step1.body: Speed, growth, attention. Adoption loops. Fast iteration. The narrative.
06-momentum.step2.title: Adoption
06-momentum.step2.body: Users committing. First-mile contracts signed. Repeat behavior emerging.
06-momentum.step3.title: Scale
06-momentum.step3.body: Volume hits the threshold where unit costs drop and leverage compounds.
06-momentum.step4.title: The Moat.
06-momentum.step4.body: Defensibility emerges from accumulated scale. Now we're hard to dislodge.
06-momentum.reframe1.label: Momentum
06-momentum.reframe1.text: Short-term speed, growth, attention. Gets you noticed and funded.
06-momentum.reframe2.label: Defensibility — the Moat
06-momentum.reframe2.text: Long-term protection from competitors. Keeps you alive once everyone else notices.

07-four-moats.source.note: NFX identifies four defensibilities that matter most. Each requires scale — and momentum is what unlocks that scale. FBX is engineered for all four.
07-four-moats.moat1.title: Network Effects
07-four-moats.moat1.def: Value grows as more participants join the network.
07-four-moats.moat1.app: More franchisees means denser delivery coverage, more installer capacity, and richer market data — which makes every additional franchise more valuable than the last.
07-four-moats.moat2.title: Economies of Scale
07-four-moats.moat2.def: Costs drop and pricing power grows with volume.
07-four-moats.moat2.app: Aggregated procurement of panels, pods, and SIPs across 137 fabs unlocks pricing no regional builder can match. Hub-and-spoke logistics compound the advantage.
07-four-moats.moat3.title: Brand
07-four-moats.moat3.def: Trust and familiarity built through repeated customer exposure.
07-four-moats.moat3.app: A standardized customer experience across every delivery market — Tesla / Carmax for housing. Repeat exposure turns "factory home" into a category we own.
07-four-moats.moat4.title: Embedding
07-four-moats.moat4.def: Becoming hard to replace through depth of integration.
07-four-moats.moat4.app: Franchisees run on FBX systems — design, permits, kit, logistics, training. Switching out is replacing the whole operating stack, not just a supplier.
07-four-moats.footer.left: Each moat compounds with scale · Scale comes from momentum

08-precedent.lede: The companies investors point to as having "unbeatable moats" today all started with raw speed and distribution. The defensibility came later — built on top of scale they earned by moving first.
08-precedent.co1.name: Amazon
08-precedent.co1.momentum: Cheapest, fastest book selection on the web. Loss-leader pricing to capture share.
08-precedent.co1.moat: Economies of scale. Massive buying volume → cost advantage no competitor can match.
08-precedent.co2.name: Airbnb
08-precedent.co2.momentum: Aggressive host onboarding, design-led trust, viral growth in cities one by one.
08-precedent.co2.moat: Network effects. Hosts and guests reinforce each other — a two-sided moat that compounds.
08-precedent.co3.name: Booking.com
08-precedent.co3.momentum: Billions in performance marketing. Repeat usage. Relentless conversion optimization.
08-precedent.co3.moat: Brand trust. The default verb for booking travel — built one transaction at a time.
08-precedent.co4.name: FBX HomeFab
08-precedent.co4.momentum: First-mover franchise rollout. AI-assisted permits. Hub-and-spoke from Day 1.
08-precedent.co4.moat: All four. Network effects across franchisees, scale economics on the kit, brand on the customer side, embedding through systems.

09-first-mile.subhead: Established. Vertically integrated sales, finance, and customer care. Think Tesla, Carmax, Carvana — for a delivered home.
09-first-mile.fn1.label: Function · A
09-first-mile.fn1.title: Financial Sector
09-first-mile.fn2.label: Function · B
09-first-mile.fn2.title: Design & Approvals
09-first-mile.fn2.body: Leverages systems, regulatory hurdles, and engineering efficiency.
09-first-mile.fn3.label: Function · C
09-first-mile.fn3.title: Sales
09-first-mile.fn3.body: Leverages domain expertise and reduced product variation.
09-first-mile.fn4.label: Function · D
09-first-mile.fn4.title: Permits
09-first-mile.cta.label: Objective
09-first-mile.cta.text: Hand the Last Mile a steady stream of homes to build & support.
09-first-mile.footer.left: Pricing controlled · Customer experience maximized · Tech-leveraged

10-kit-of-parts.lede: A standardized, factory-built component system — assembled in the field by trained, regional teams.
10-kit-of-parts.products: Products: ADUs · High-Density Multi-Family · Bathroom Pods · SIPs
10-kit-of-parts.part1.label: Component · 01
10-kit-of-parts.part1.title: Structural Panels
10-kit-of-parts.part1.body: AI-assisted, pre-cut panels — field installed by trained crews with engineered placement guides.
10-kit-of-parts.part2.label: Component · 02
10-kit-of-parts.part2.title: Bathroom Pods
10-kit-of-parts.part2.body: The technical, plumbing-heavy work — built in a controlled factory environment, dropped in place on site.
10-kit-of-parts.part3.label: Component · 03
10-kit-of-parts.part3.title: Warehousing
10-kit-of-parts.part3.body: Cost efficiency at scale — aggregated procurement and regional staging across the FBX Core network.
10-kit-of-parts.objective.label: Objective
10-kit-of-parts.objective.text: Regulatory clearance · Efficient design · Best product · Best distribution.

11-distribution.quote.text:
"Distribution excellence is the key.
Bring in parts, package, ship to the
Last Mile — let them earn on the
efficiency we created."

12-workforce.subhead: Behind every structure is a diverse team — engineers, laborers, surveyors, project managers. They want to scale. They demand support. We empower their rise.
12-workforce.stat1.label: Franchise Service Radius
12-workforce.stat2.label: Fabs at Year 10 (national)

15-economics.core.name: Core
15-economics.fab.name: Fab-Home
15-economics.tot.label: Network Totals at Y10
15-economics.footer.left: Numbers are model-driven · Live in the Expansion Map (slide 14)

16-objections.obj1.q: How big can this get? Homes per year?
16-objections.obj1.a: See the model and the map (slide 14). The plan is 149,490 homes delivered by Year 10 at a 43,980/yr run-rate. We can dial it more aggressive or more conservative — the model recalcs live.
16-objections.obj2.q: What is your moat? How do you fend off copycats?
16-objections.obj2.a: Wrong question at this stage. See slides 06–08 — moats emerge from scale, and scale comes from momentum. We're engineered for all four NFX defensibilities (network effects, scale economics, brand, embedding) once we hit volume. First-mover speed is the strategy.
16-objections.obj3.q: Are you national, or competing with Lennar / Champion?
16-objections.obj3.a: Adding to the market — demand is enormous. Our edge is speed and cost of capital, which is why the moat matters more than the comparison.

17-ask.sub: We're raising $4M for FBX Core. Each Fab-Home franchise is funded independently — $900K franchise fee, $450K infrastructure. The model scales linearly in capital, exponentially in delivery.
17-ask.n1.label: FBX Core · Series A
17-ask.n2.label: Per Franchise · Fee
17-ask.n3.label: Per Franchise · Infrastructure
